summary: First iteration end-to-end testing for Avtomoyka Car Wash Management System. Backend: 29/29 pytest tests passed sequentially covering auth (admin login, brute-force protection 401, /auth/me, logout cookie clear), user CRUD (create kassir/hodim, list, role 403 for non-admin), services (list defaults, create with admin, 403 for hodim), full order lifecycle (create -> navbatda -> yuvilmoqda by hodim -> tugallandi -> payment by kassir -> tolandi), all 4 payment methods (naqd/karta/click/payme), role-based filtering (hodim sees only assigned, kassir sees completed), dashboard stats per role, reports (today report, excel export with valid xlsx content, close-day), and 403 checks for unauthorized endpoints. Frontend: Admin/Kassir/Hodim login flows verified, role-based sidebar correctly hides Xizmatlar/Foydalanuvchilar/Hisobotlar for hodim, hides Xizmatlar/Foydalanuvchilar for kassir. All key data-testid attributes are present (add-order-button, add-service-button, add-user-button, export-excel-button, close-day-button, login inputs, logout). Dashboard renders correct stats per role.

minor.issue: Returns 500 instead of 400 on race condition (two concurrent close attempts). Re-close path returns 400 correctly when ordered, but parallel xdist runs intermittently triggered 500. Likely missing exception handling or duplicate insert.
Backend re-resolves user via email lookup on every order list/status change. Consider passing user_id from JWT directly. Not functionally broken.
Race condition under concurrent failed login increments — locked_until may be set inconsistently. Not blocking.

action_items: Optional: harden /api/reports/close-day with unique index on report_date + try/except to return 400 instead of 500 on duplicate concurrent close.
Optional: pass user _id in JWT payload to avoid re-querying users collection on every order request.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 970 lines and mixes auth, users, services, orders, dashboard, reports, excel export — should be split into routers/modules (>700 lines threshold).
Dead code in get_services: services = await db.services.find({}, {'_id': 0}).sort('name',1).to_list(1000) loop then redundant cursor loop. Remove the first block.

PATCH /orders/{id}/status returns order_dict containing raw mongo fields via **{k: v for k, v in updated_order.items() if k != '_id'} — `_id` is excluded but other internal fields (created_at) leak; acceptable but inconsistent with OrderResponse model.
POST /auth/register returns user dict without password_hash but uses {'_id': 0, 'password_hash': 0} then sets user['id']; could simplify with Pydantic response model.
CORS allow_credentials=True combined with allow_origins='*' (from CORS_ORIGINS env) would be unsafe — currently overridden by FRONTEND_URL env. Verify in prod that FRONTEND_URL is set, not '*'.
No pagination on /api/orders, /api/users — to_list(1000) is a hard cap. For scale add skip/limit query params.
JWT secret stored in .env (good) but no token rotation/blacklist on logout (just deletes cookie). Acceptable for MVP.
test_credentials: admin@avtomoyka.uz/Admin123!, kassir@avtomoyka.uz/Kassir123!, hodim@avtomoyka.uz/Hodim123! — all seeded and verified working.
seed_data_creation: Created kassir and hodim users via /api/auth/register. Created multiple test orders for license plate TEST123AA, TESTKARTA, TESTCLICK, TESTPAYME with all 4 payment methods exercised.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py runs reliably with -n 0 (sequential). The configured pytest.ini -n 2 --dist loadscope causes intermittent failures in TestOrdersWorkflow because session-scoped sessions (kassir/hodim) are recreated per worker and the order workflow is class-sequential. Use `python -m pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -n 0` for deterministic runs. All admin/kassir/hodim test accounts already exist in DB. Frontend at https://cleancar-pro-2.preview.emergentagent.com is fully functional. The system is production-ready for MVP.
rca of the issue: No critical issues. Minor 500 on /reports/close-day under concurrency: missing exception handling around duplicate insert OR race between existence check and insert. Mitigation: add unique index on report_date in daily_reports collection and wrap insert in try/except DuplicateKeyError -> 400.
